items.prompt: For non-empty sets A and B, which statement is correct about the cartesian product?
items.answer: In general, A x B is not equal to B x A

expected.correct: In general, A x B is not equal to B x A
options: In general, A x B is not equal to B x A
A x B always equals B x A
A x B is a single ordered pair
A x B is empty whenever A and B are non-empty

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch02 Sec 2.2 Example 2 / Summary (A x B is not B x A)

items.prompt: A function f is defined by f(x) = 2x - 5. The value of f(7) is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch02 Exercise 2.3 Q3 (evaluate f(x) = 2x - 5)

items.prompt: Find the domain of the rational function f(x) = (x^2 + 3x + 5)/(x^2 - 5x + 4). Explain how you decide which real numbers to exclude.

expected.rubric: A rational function f(x)/g(x) is defined wherever the denominator g(x) is not zero, so the domain excludes the zeros of the denominator. Here x^2 - 5x + 4 = (x - 4)(x - 1), which is zero at x = 1 and x = 4. Hence the domain is R minus {1, 4} (all real numbers except 1 and 4).
markPoints: A rational function is undefined where the denominator is zero
Factor x^2 - 5x + 4 = (x - 4)(x - 1), giving zeros x = 1 and x = 4

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch02 Example 21 (domain of a rational function)

items.prompt: Let f(x) = x^2 and g(x) = 2x + 1. Then (f - g)(2) equals:
The graph of the identity function f(x) = x is:
items.answer: a straight line passing through the origin

expected.correct: a straight line passing through the origin
options: a straight line passing through the origin
a line parallel to the x-axis
a line parallel to the y-axis

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch02 Sec 2.4.1(i) (identity function graph)
